title: cymbalaria aequitriloba subsp. fragilis
intro: Cymbalaria aequitriloba subsp. fragilis, also known as the 'fragile toothwort', is a rare and lesser-known species within the cymbalaria genus, characterized by its unique, tooth-like leaves and delicate appearance. Traditionally, the fragrant roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their purported medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Cymbalaria aequitriloba subsp. fragilis include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of its roots, which can be consumed as teas or added to various remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
